title: Aaron
description: The eldest son of Amram and Jochebed (Ex 6:20). Moses and Miriam were his siblings (Ex 2:1,4; 4:14; 7:7). He married Elisheba by whom he had four sons: Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar (Ex 6:23; 1 Chr 2:10). Aaron and his sons were consecrated to the priest office (Lev 8-9). Aaron died on the top of Mount Hor, being 123 years old (Num 20:23-29; Deut 32:50; cf. Deut 10:6). Aaron appears in the NT only in Hebrews (5:4; 7:11; 9:4).

title: Abba
description: An Aramaic word for “father”, found three times in the New Testament (Mk 14:36; Rom 8:15; Gal 4:6). The word expresses warm affection and filial confidence. It has no perfect equivalent in other languages
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Abel
description: The second son of Adam and Eve (Gen 4:2), the first martyr of the human race (Gen 4:3-8).

title: Abraham
description: The first of the great patriarchs of Israel. He is known in the NT as “the friend of God” (Jam 2:23), “faithful Abraham” (Gal 3:9), “the father of us all” (Rom 4:16).

title: Abraham's bosom
description: This refers to the custom of reclining on couches at table, which was prevalent among the Jews, an arrangement which brought the head of one person almost into the bosom of the one who sat or reclined above him. To “be in Abraham’s bosom” (Lk 16:22,23) thus meant to enjoy happiness and rest (Mt 8:11; Luk 16:23) at the banquet in Paradise
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Absalom
description: David’s son who staged a coup d’etat against his father but was finally killed by Joab (2 Sam 15–18).

title: Aceldama
description: The field purchased with the money obtained by Judas for betraying Jesus. The word means “field of blood.” It was previously called “the potter’s field” (Mt 27:7,8; Acts 1:19), and was appropriated as the burial place for strangers

title: Adam
description: Originally a Babylonian word which means ‘red’, used later as a generic name for man, having the same meaning in the Hebrew and the Assyrian languages. It was the name given to the first man (Gen 1:27; 2:17). Adam and Eve are presented in the Bible as the progenitors of the whole human race (Acts 17:26; cf. Rom 5:12-12; 1 Cor 15:22-49)
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Adonijah
description: The fourth son of David (2 Sam 3:4), heir-apparent, who was put to death by Solomon when he came to the throne (1 Kings 2:13-25).

title: Agrippa 1
description: The grandson of Herod the Great, and son of Aristobulus and Bernice. After becoming the ruler over the whole of Palestine, he persecuted Christians, martyred the Apostle James and imprisoned Peter (Acts 12:1-4).

title: Agrippa 2
description: The son of Agrippa I and the brother of Bernice and Drusilla. He was the ruler of Palestine before whom Paul delivered his speech recorded in Acts 26.

title: Ahab
description: The wicked king of Israel who married Jezebel, a pagan who promoted Baal worship in Israel (1 Kings 16–22). Prophets Elijah and Micaiah reprimanded Ahab and his wife for their evil deeds.

title: Alabaster
description: It was the precious perfume with which a woman anointed the head of Jesus as he sat at supper in the house of Simon the leper (Mt 26:6-16; Mk 14:3-11; Lk 7:36-50). Jesus’ dead body was covered with perfumes of myrrh and aloes (Jn 19:39). Alabaster jars were made from a stone found near Alabastron in Egypt and from this came the name of the perfume and its jar.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Alien
description: A foreigner or person born in another country and therefore not entitled to the rights and privileges of the country where he resides (Lev 22:10; Ps 39:12; Lev 19:33,34; Deut 10:19; Deut 23:3-8). In the NT this term is used to denote persons who have no interest in Christ (Eph 2:12).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Alleluia
description: The Greek form (Rev 19:1,3,4,6) of the Hebrew ‘Hallelujah’ (i.e. Praise the Lord). It is used in Halleluiah Psalms
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Amen
description: This Hebrew word means “so be it”. Jesus used it frequently to give emphasis to his words, where it is translated “verily”. It is used as an epithet of the Lord Jesus Christ (Rev 3:14). It was common for the general audience to say “Amen” at the close of the prayer (1 Cor 14:16).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Anathema
description: In the OT this word points to ‘a person/thing accursed’, while in the NT it implies execration or curse. This term is derived from Greek and it means: a) to consecrate or devote; and b) to exterminate. Its Hebrew equivalent is harem which means ‘excommunication’ or ‘boycott.’ Any object dedicated to the Lord could not be redeemed (Num 18:14; Lev 27:28,29); and thus originated the idea of ‘exterminating’ connected with the word. The Hebrew harem is frequently used for the extermination of idolatrous nations. It had a wide range of application. The anathema or herem was a person or thing irrevocably dedicated to God (Lev 27:21,28); and dedicated persons or things could not be ransomed (Lev 27:29). The word therefore carried the idea of being dedicated to destruction (Num 21:2,3; Josh 6:17); hence generally it meant a thing accursed. In Deut 7:26 an idol is called a herem/anathema, a thing accursed. In the New Testament this word always implies execration or curse (Acts 23:12,14,21; 1 Cor. 12:3; Gal 1:8-9; 1 Cor 16:22).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Anathoth
description: The birthplace of Jeremiah (Jer 1:1; 11:21-23; 29:27; 32:7-9). It was one of the cities of refuge (Josh 21:18).

title: Ancient of Days
description: An expression applied to Yahweh in the vision of Daniel (7:9,13,22) in the sense that God is eternal in contrast to all earthly kings
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Andrew
description: One of the twelve apostles of Jesus and the brother of Simon Peter (Mt 4:18; Mk 1:16-17). Before becoming Jesus’ follower, Andrew was a disciple of John the Baptist (Jn 1:40).

title: Annas
description: The Jewish high priest from AD 7 to 14 in Jerusalem.

title: Antichrist
description: The term means a rival Christ (1 Jn 2:18,22; 4:3; 2 Jn 1:7). In the Book of Daniel this term refers to “little horn” of the “king of fierce countenance” (Dan 7:24,25; 8:23-25). In the Gospel of Matthew ‘antichrist’ is applied to ‘false Christs’ (Mt 24:5,23,24). The term also stands for the “man of sin” (2 Thess 2:3,4,8-10).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Antioch
description: There are two places named ‘Antioch’ mentioned in the Bible: ‘Antioch on Orontes’ and ‘Antioch of Pisidia’. Antioch of Syria or Antioch on the river Orontes was the metropolis of Syria and it became the capital of the Roman province in Asia. This was the third important city after Rome and Alexandria in the Roman Empire. The followers of Christ were named “Christians” for the first time here (Acts 11:26). It was intimately connected with the early Church (Acts 6:5; 11:19,27,28,30; 12:25; 15:22-35; Gal 2:11,12). Starting their missionary journey from ‘Antioch on Orontes’ (Acts 13:1-3), Paul and Barnabas reached ‘Antioch of Pisidia’ in their first missionary journey (Acts 13:13-52; 14:21).

title: Antiochus Epiphanes 4
description: The persecutor of Jews in Jerusalem between 171 and 164 BC (Dan 11:21-32).

title: Antipas, Herod
description: The son of Herod the Great and the tetrarch of Galilee and Perea (4 BC–AD 39).

title: Antonia
description: A fortress in Jerusalem at the north-west corner of the temple area called “the castle/barracks” (Acts 21:34,37). From the stairs of this fortress Paul delivered his famous speech to the Jewish crowd (Acts 22:1-21).

title: Apocrypha
description: A collective name for those books which are not considered part of the Canonical Bible but have value and interest for Christians in reading them. The word ‘apocrypha’ is the plural form of the Greek word ‘apocryphon’ which means ‘hidden away’ or ‘secret’.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Apollos
description: A Jew from Alexandria, well versed in the Scriptures and eloquent in speaking, who preached the Gospel (1 Cor 1:12; 3:4-7,22).

title: Apostle
description: The word is derived from Greek and it means ‘person sent by another’, ‘a messenger’ or ‘envoy’. This title has been traditionally used for the twelve disciples whom Jesus chose in order to be with him and to be sent (Mt 10:1-5; Mk 3: 14; Lk 6:13; 9:1). We have four lists of the apostles, one by each of the synoptic evangelists (Mt 10:2-4; Mk 3:16; Lk 6:14), and one in the Acts (1:13).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Aquila
description: Aquila and his wife Priscilla were companions of Paul in tent-making as well as in preaching the gospel (cf. Acts 18:18; 1 Cor 16:19; Rom 16:3; 2 Tim 4:19)

title: Aram
description: The earlier name of Syria; Damascus was its capital. Aramaens were the same as Syrians (Gen 25:20; 31:20,24; Deut 26:5).

title: Ararat
description: The mountains on which the ark of Noah rested after the flood subsided (Gen 8:4). In Jer 51:27, Ararat denotes the central or southern portion of Armenia.

title: Archelaus
description: The son of Herod the Great and the ethnarch of Idumea, Judea, and Samaria (4 BC–AD 6).

title: Areopagus
description: A rocky height to the west of the Acropolis at Athens, denoting the court of justice held on the south-east summit of the height. Paul delivered his memorable address there (Acts 17:22-31).

title: Ariel
description: It was a symbolic name for Jerusalem (Is 29:1,2,7) with the meaning “victorious under God”, and in Ezek 43:15,16 with the meaning of the altar of burnt offerings.

title: Aristarchus
description: A Macedonian from Thessalonica (Acts 27:2; 20:4), a traveling companion of Paul (Acts 19:29) and Paul’s “fellow-prisoner” at Rome (Col 4:10; Philem 1:24).

title: Ark of the Covenant
description: A wooden box covered with gold and kept in the ‘holy of holies’ (1 Kings 8:8). There was nothing in the ark except the two tablets of stone (1 Kings 8:9). Because of Exod 16:33 and Num 17:10 it was believed that a jar of manna and Aaron’s rod were also inside the ark (Heb 9:4).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Assyria
description: The ancient dominant kingdom of Mesopotamia; the name comes from the city of Asshur on the Tigris. Assyria became the dominant power of the region, putting an end to the northern kingdom of Israel in 722 BC and deporting its people to Assyria (2 Kings 17:1-6,24; 18:7,9). Assyria attacked the southern kingdom of Judah in 705– 701 BC but could not capture Jerusalem (2 Kings 18:13; 19:37; Is 36–37).

title: Athens
description: The capital of Greece and the centre of Greek civilization, literature and art. Paul visited Athens during his missionary journey (Acts 17:15; 17:22-31).

title: Augustus
description: The name given by the Senate to the first Roman emperor, Julius Caesar Octavianus (BC 27). It meant ‘majesty’ or ‘venerable’ and the succeeding emperors used this as a title. Jesus was born during his reign in Rome (Lk 2:1).

title: Azazel
description: The exact connotation of this term is not clear. There are some suggestions: a) a place in the wilderness to which a goat was sent on the Day of Atonement; b) the name of an evil spirit or of Satan (Lev 16:26).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Baal
description: The term denoting the principal Phoenician god, but each locality had its special Baal. The sun-god, under the general title of Baal (= lord) was the chief object of worship of the Canaanites. The Israelites were tempted to go after the worship of this pagan god (Judg 2:11; 10:10; 1 Kings 18:18; Jer 2:2,3; Hos 2:17).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Baal-peor
description: The god of the Moabites (Num 25:3; 31:16; Josh 22:17).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Babel Tower
description: The towers built in the land of Shinar/Babylon after the Deluge as a sign of human pride (Gen 11:1-9).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Babylon
description: An ancient kingdom in Mesopotamia near the Euphrates that became strong after the fall of the Assyria. In 598 and 597 BC Babylon attacked the kingdom of Judah and deported its people into exile (2 Kings 24–25). The name ‘Babylon’ is used symbolically in the Book of Revelation (Rev 14:8; 16:19; 17:5; 18:2).

title: Balaam
description: A pagan prophet hired by king Balak to curse the Israelites. Instead of cursing, Balaam prophesied about Israel’s glorious future (Num 22–24).

title: Barnabas
description: A native of Cyprus (Acts 4:36-37) and Paul’s missionary companion for some time (Acts 13–15).

title: Bartholomew
description: One of the twelve apostles and the son of Tolomai (Mt 10:3; Acts 1:13). Some believe that he was the same as Nathanael.

title: Bartimaeus
description: One of the two blind beggars of Jericho who was cured by Jesus (Mk 10:46; Mt 20:30)

title: Bashan
description: This place extended from Gilead in the south to Hermon in the north, and from the Jordan on the west to Salcah on the east. It was known for its green pastures, oak trees and good cows (Ezek 39:18; Ps 22:12; Is 2:13; Ezek 27:6; Zech 11:2; Amos 4:1; Jer 50:19).

title: Bastard
description: An illegitimate offspring; bastards were not allowed to enter the assembly of the Lord (Deut 23:2; Judg 11:1-7). In Heb. 12:8 the word denotes those who do not share the privileges of God’s children.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Bathsheba
description: The wife of Uriah, who later became David’s wife (2 Sam 11:27) and gave birth to Solomon (2 Sam 12:24).

title: Beersheba
description: A place in southern Israel where a well was dug by Abraham; the name means ‘well of the oath’ because Abraham and Abimelech swore an oath (Gen 21:31). Isaac also used the well (Gen 26:31-33; 21:33–22:1,19; 26:33; 28:10).

title: Belial
description: The words means worthlessness (Deut 13:13; Judg 19: 22; 20:13; 1 Sam 1:16; 2:12). It is translated “wicked” in Deut 15:9; Ps 41:8; 101:3; Prov 6:12, etc.). In 2 Cor 6:15 it is used as a name for Satan as the personification of all that is evil.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Benjamin
description: Jacob’s son by Rachel and the younger brother of Joseph (Gen 35:18). Benjamin formed one of the twelve tribes of Israel (Gen 49:27; Deut 33:12; Josh 18:21).

title: Bethany
description: A village on the south-eastern slope of the Mount of Olives (Mk 11:1), about 2 miles east of Jerusalem. It derived its name from the number of palm trees which grew there and the word means ‘house of dates’. It was the residence of Lazarus and his sisters (Mt 21:17; 26:6; Mk 11:11, 12; 14:3; Lk 24:50; Jn 11:1; 12:1).

title: Bethel
description: A place about 10 miles north of Jerusalem, originally a Canaanite city known as Luz (Gen 28: 19). Jacob had a vision here (Gen 28:10- 22; 35:1-15).

title: Bethlehem
description: The birthplace of Jesus about 5 miles south of Jerusalem. It was originally called Ephrath (Gen 35:16, 19; 48:7; Ruth 4:11). It was also called Beth-lehem of Ephratah (Mic 5:1), Beth-lehem in Judah (1 Sam 17:12), and “the city of David” (Lk 2:4). Rachel died and was buried there (Gen 48:7). Beit-lehem means ‘house of bread’ whereas Beit-lahm means ‘house of flesh’.

title: Bethphage
description: A place on the Mount of Olives, very close to Bethany and its name means ‘house of the unripe fig.’ It was once on the road from Jerusalem to Jericho (Mt 21:1; Mk 11:1; Lk 19:29).

title: Bethsaida
description: A town in Galilee on the west side of the sea of Tiberias, the native place of Peter, Andrew, and Philip (Mk 6:45; Jn 1:44; 12:21). Jesus healed a blind man here (Mk 8:22-26). The name ‘Bethsaida’ means ‘house of fish’

title: Bethzatha
description: A pool with five porches, close to the Sheep Gate or market in Jerusalem (Neh 3:1). Jesus healed a paralytic there (Jn 5:1-15).

title: Birthright
description: This word denotes the special privileges and advantages belonging to the firstborn son among the Jews. Every first born belonged to the Lord and had to be redeemed by the Levites (Num 3:12,13; 8:18). The firstborn son had the right to inherit a double portion of the paternal inheritance (Deut 21:15-17).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Bithynia
description: A province in Asia Minor where Christianity was planted early on (1 Pet 1:1). Paul was prevented by the Spirit from entering this province (Acts 16:7).

title: Blasphemy
description: Speaking evil of God (Ps 74:18; Is 52:5; Rom 2:24; Rev 13:1,6; 16:9,11,21). Any kind of calumny or evil speaking or abuse is also considered as blasphemy (1 Kings 21:10; Acts 13:45; 18:6, etc.). Jews accused Jesus of blasphemy when he claimed to be the Son of God (Mt 26:65; Jn 10:36; Mt 9:3; Mk 2:7). Blasphemy against the Holy Spirit (Mt 12:31,32; Mk 3:28,29; Lk 12:10) is regarded as a continued and obstinate rejection of the Gospel, and hence is an unpardonable sin
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Boanerges
description: The term means ‘sons of thunder’. It was a nickname given by Jesus to James and John (Mk 3:17) on account of their fervid and impetuous temper (Lk 9:54).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Caesarea Philippi
description: A city on the north-eastern part of Israel about 120 miles from Jerusalem. Tetrarch Herod Philip rebuilt this city and named it Caesarea Philippi, partly after his own name, and partly after that of the emperor Tiberius Caesar. The Apostle Peter made his confession of faith in this place (Mt 16:13-20; Mk 8:27-30; Lk 9:18-22).

title: Cain
description: The firstborn son of Adam and Eve, who killed his brother Abel (Gen 4).

title: Calvary
description: The site of Jesus’ crucifixion (Mk 15:22; Mt 27:33; Lk 23:33). The English word ‘Calvary’ is derived from the Latin word ‘Calvaria’ translating the Greek word Kranion, which was a translation of the Hebrew word ‘Gulgotha’ which means ‘skull’. The name arose from the fact that the place looked like a skull.

title: Cana
description: The village eight miles away from Nazareth, where Jesus turned water into wine during a wedding dinner (Jn 2:1-11; cf. 4:46). Nathanael was from Cana (Jn 21:2).

title: Canaan
description: The land to the west of the Jordan and the Dead Sea (Deut 11:30; Gen 10:19; 17:8; Num 13:29; 34:8). It was taken over by the Israelite tribes as their promised land.

title: Canon
description: This Greek word means a reed or cane. By interpretation it meant something straight, or something to keep things straight, and hence also a rule or something ruled or measured. When applied to the Scriptures, the word ‘canon’ denotes the sacred writings that contain the authoritative rule of faith and doctrine.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Capernaum
description: A place 15.5 kms away from Tiberias. The original name of this place was Kefar Nahum (village of Nahum). Until the fifth century there was a Jewish Christian community here together with a Jewish population. It is not mentioned in the Old Testament. After Jesus’ expulsion from Nazareth (Mt 4:13-16; Lk 4:16-31), Capernaum became his “own city” (Mk 2:1; 3:19-21).

title: Catholic Epistles
description: Seven Epistles addressed to Christians in general and not to any Church or person in particular: the Epistle of James, two Epistles of Peter, three Epistles of John, and the Epistle of Jude
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Centurion
description: A Roman officer in command of a hundred soldiers (Mk 15:39,44,45). Cornelius was a centurion (Acts 10:1,22) and other centurions are also mentioned (Mt 8:5,8,13; Lk 7:2,6; Acts 21:32; 22:25,26; 23:17,23; 24:23; 27:1,6,11,31,43; 28:16).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Chorazin
description: A place north of Capernaum; Jesus reprimanded the people there for their incredulity despite seeing his miracles (Mt 11:21; Lk 10:13).

title: Christ
description: This English word is derived from the Greek word ‘Christos’ which is equivalent to the Hebrew word ‘Mashiha’, which means ‘anointed’. ‘Christ’ was not the surname of Jesus. The name ‘Christ’ is added to Jesus as he was the Messiah anointed to carry out the redemptive work for humanity (Acts 17:3; 18:5; Mt 22:42).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Church
description: In the NT the word ‘church’ is the translation of the Greek word ‘ecclesia’ (Mt 16:18; 18:18) and its Hebrew equivalent is ‘kahal’, and both words mean ‘assembly’. Some see the origin of the English word ‘church’ from the Greek word ‘kuriakon’ (i.e., ‘the Lord’s house’), which meant a place of worship in ancient times. In the NT ‘Church’ means the ‘body of the redeemed’ (Eph 5:23,25,27,29; Heb 12:23), a local Christian community (Rom 16:5; Col 4:15), all local communities of a city together (Acts 13:1; 1 Cor 1:2; Rev 2:1), and all Christians all over the world (1 Cor 15:9; Gal 1:13; Mt 16:18)
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Circumcision
description: A religious ceremony in Judaism and Islam during which all or part of the foreskin from the penis is removed (Gen 17:10-14, 22-27). Although some Jewish Christians wanted to make circumcision a requirement for all Christians, Paul and his companions vehemently resisted it and hence it did not get endorsement in the Church (Acts 15:1-35; Gal 6:12)
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Colossae
description: A city of Phrygia about 12 miles above Laodicea. Epaphras was the founder of the Colossian Church (Col 1:7; 4:12).

title: Communion
description: It is ‘koinonia’ or fellowship with God (Gen 18:17-33; Ex 33:9-11; Num 12:7,8), fellowship between Christ and his people (Jn 14:23), by the Spirit (2 Cor 13:14; Phil 2:1), of believers with one another (Eph 4:1-6). The Eucharist is called ‘communion’ (1 Cor 10:16,17), because this is the sacrament that imparts fellowship between Christ and his disciples, and of the disciples with one another
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Corban
description: A Hebrew word adopted into New Testament Greek, and it means a gift or offering consecrated to God (Mk 7:11).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Corinth
description: A Greek city about 48 miles west of Athens. Paul founded the Church there and stayed there for eighteen months (Acts 18:1-18).

title: Cornelius
description: A Roman centurion with his residence at Caesarea, who embraced Christian faith at the preaching of Peter (Acts 10).

title: Covenant
description: A treaty or contract or agreement between two parties (Gen 9:11; 15:18; Ex 20; Deut 5; 2 Sam 7; Jer 31:31-34; 34:18,19). The Hebrew word for covenant is berith while its Greek word is diatheke (testament). This word is used of a covenant between two people (Gen 21:32); between two tribes or two nations (1 Sam 11:1; Josh 9:6,15). It also refers to God’s revelation of Himself in the way of promise or of favour to people. There are four main covenants of Yahweh in the OT: with Noah (Gen 9: 11); with Abraham (Gen 15:18); with Moses (Ex 20; Deut 5); and with David (2 Sam 7).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Cross
description: The Roman form of execution used for the crucifixion of Christ (Eph 2:16; Heb 12:2; 1 Cor 1:17,18; Gal 5:11; 6:12,14; Phil 3:18). After AD 313 the cross became the emblem of Christianity.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Crucifixion
description: This was regarded among Jews as the most horrible form of death especially because of the curse in Deut 21:23. This punishment began by subjecting the sufferer to scourging, and then the condemned one carried his own cross to the place of execution which was outside the city. Jesus was given the punishment of crucifixion. Some people point out the last memorable words of Jesus on the cross: (1) Lk 23:34; (2) Lk 23:43; (3) Jn 19:26-27; (4) Mt 27:46; Mk 15:34; (5) Jn 19:28; (6) Jn 19:30; (7) Lk 23:46
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Cyprus
description: The native place of Barnabas (Acts 4: 36); Paul and Barnabas went to Cyprus in their first missionary journey (Acts 13:4-13).

title: Cyrene
description: A city (now Tripoli) in upper Libya in north Africa. Simon, who bore our Lord’s cross, was a native of this place (Mt 27:32; Mk 15:21).

title: Damascus
description: The capital of Syria (Is 7:8; 17:3); It was on his way to Damascus that Paul had an encounter with Jesus which led to his conversion (Acts 9:1-25).

title: Daniel
description: The main character of the apocalyptic Book of Daniel, not a historical figure.

title: David
description: The most illustrious king of the united kingdom of Israel. He was the eighth son of Jesse from Bethlehem (1 Sam 16–2 Sam 24; 1 Kings 1–2).

title: Dead Sea
description: Known by different names in the Bible, such as the “salt sea” (Gen 14:3; Num 34:12), the “sea of the plain” (Deut 3:17), the “east sea” (Ezek 47:18; Joel 2:20), and simply “the sea” (Ezek 47:8). The Dead Sea water contains 24.6 per cent of mineral salts which is about seven times as much as in ordinary sea water, and hence no living beings can exist in this water. The Dead Sea is 792 meters below sea level, 408 meters deep, 76 kilometers long and 17 kilometers wide.

title: Decalogue
description: The Ten Commandments that Yahweh gave to Israel through Moses (Ex 20:3-17; Deut 5:6-21). The New Testament refers to the Decalogue several times (Mt 5:17,18,19; Mk 10:19; Lk 18:20; Rom 7:7,8; 13:9; 1 Tim 1:9,10).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Decapolis
description: A region of ten cities on the east and south-east of the Sea of Galilee (Mt 4:25; Mk 5:20; 7:31).

title: Derbe
description: Paul evangelized there during his various missionary journeys (Acts 14:20-21; 16:1; 18:23; 20:4).

title: Deuteronomy
description: The fifth book of the Pentateuch and of the Bible
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Devil
description: This term is derived from the Greek word ‘diabolos’ which means ‘a slanderer’, the arch-enemy of man’s spiritual interest (Job 1:6; Rev 2:10; Zech 3:1). He is called also “the accuser of the brethren” (Rev 12: 10). Jesus healed several people who were victims of demoniacal possession (Mt 12:25-30; Mk 5:1-20; Lk 4:35; 10:18, etc.)
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Easter
description: The Christian feast celebrating Jesus’ resurrection from the dead. The word ‘Easter’ is derived from a Saxon word ‘Eostre’.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Ecclesiastes
description: One of the Wisdom Books of the Bible.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Egypt
description: The land of Pharaohs who oppressed the Israelites as slaves; the land of the River Nile and the Pyramids; Egypt had a very ancient civilization

title: Elijah
description: One of the prominent non-literary prophets of Israel who fought for defending Yahwism (1 Kings 17–19; 21:17-29; 2 Kings 1:1–2:14).

title: Elisha
description: One of the prominent non-literary prophets of Israel (2 Kings 2–8) and a disciple of Elijah (1 Kings 19:16-19).

title: Elizabeth
description: The wife of Zechariah and the mother of John the Baptist (Lk 1:5–80).

title: Emmanuel / Immanuel
description: The word means ‘God with us’. This term is from the messianic prophecy of Isaiah 7:14 and 8:8.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Ephesians
description: One of the Pauline Letters, addressed to the Ephesians.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Ephesus
description: The capital of the Roman province of Asia (in modern Turkey). Paul evangelized in Ephesus and founded the Church there (Acts 19).

title: Ephraim
description: Joseph’s second son (Gen 41:52; 46:20) and one of the 12 tribes

title: Ephratah
description: The ancient name of Bethlehem (Ruth 1:2; 1 Sam 17:12)

title: Esau
description: The firstborn son of Isaac and Rebekah and the twin brother of Jacob (Gen 25:25).

title: Esther
description: A Jewess named Hadassah, the main character of the book of Esther, who is said to have saved Persian Jews from destruction.

title: Ethiopia
description: A country which lay to the south of Egypt, Ethiopia was the Greek word for the Hebrew word ‘Cush’ (Gen 2:13;2 Kings 19:9; Esther 1:1; Job 28:19; Ps 68:31; 87:4).

title: Euphrates
description: One of the two great rivers of Mesopotamia (Gen 2:14). It is generally called in the Bible simply “the river” (Ex 23:31) or “the great river” (Deut 1:7).

title: Eutychus
description: The young man of Troas who fell in sleep from the open window of the third floor of the house where Paul was preaching. Paul raised him back to life (Acts 20:9-12).

title: Evangelists
description: Preachers of the Gospel (Eph 4:11; Acts 21:8; 8:4,40) as well as the writers of the four Gospels.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Eve
description: The wife of Adam (Gen 2:18-25) and the mother of all human beings (Gen 3:20; 4:1).

title: Ezekiel
description: One of the three Major Prophets (623–570 BC). There is a book in the Bible in his name

title: Frontlets / Phylacteries / tefflin
description: Interpreting the texts of Exod 13:16; Deut 6:8, and 11:18, Jews began the custom of tying on their foreheads and arms verses of the law (Deut 6:4-9; 11:18-21; Exod 12:2-10; 13:11-21), written on strips of parchment and kept in small boxes. These were known as ‘frontlets’ (Mt 23:5)
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Galatia
description: A Roman province in the central region of Asia Minor (in modern Turkey). Paul preached the Gospel there (Acts 16:6; 18:23; Gal 4:13).

title: Galilee
description: The region in western Palestine. The name ‘Galilee’ means ‘circuit’

title: Gehenna
description: A valley to the south of Jerusalem known originally as “the valley of the sons of Hinnom” where the idolatrous Jews offered their children in sacrifice to Molech (2 Chron 28:3; 33:6; Jer 7:31; 19:2-6). This valley later became the common receptacle for all the refuse of the city and a fire was always burning the filth thrown there. It became the image of the place of everlasting destruction (Mt 5:22,29,30; 10:28; 18:9; 23:15,33; Mk 9:43,45,47; Lk 12:5).

title: Gerizim
description: A mountain of Samaria about 3,000 feet above the Mediterranean

title: Gethsemane
description: An olive yard at the foot of the Mount of Olives where Jesus used to go to with his disciples when he was in Jerusalem (Lk 22:39). The word ‘Gethsemane’ means ‘oil press’.

title: Gihon
description: A spring of water in the south of Jerusalem which is described as one of the four rivers of Eden (Gen 2:13).

title: Gilead
description: A mountainous region east of Jordan (Gen 31:21,25; 37:25). The region of Gilead was rich in spices and aromatic gums and thus it became known for the balm of Gilead (Gen 37:25; Jer 8:22; 46:11; Ezek 27:17).

title: Golgotha
description: The name of the place where Jesus was crucified (Mt 27:33; Mk 15:22; Jn 19:17).

title: Gomorrah
description: One of the five cities of the plain of Siddim destroyed by fire (Gen 10:19; 13:10; 19:24,28). The word means ‘submersion.’

title: Gospel
description: A word meaning “God’s spell”, i.e. word of God. It comes from the Greek evangelion, i.e. “good message”.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Greece
description: An ancient country with a great civilization. It is known as Javan in the Bible (Gen 10:2-5). Paul evangelised in several cities of Greece such as Philippi, Thessalonica and Athens.

title: Habakkuk
description: One of the Minor Prophets who exercised his ministry after the fall of Nineveh in 612 BC.

title: Hades
description: The place of the dead equivalent to the Hebrew Sheol, the common abode of the departed (Gen 42:38; Ps 139:8; Hos 13:14; Is 14:9).

title: Hagar
description: Sarah’s Egyptian maid by whom Abraham had his son Ishmael (Gen 16).

title: Haggai
description: One of the Minor Prophets who exercised his ministry in Jerusalem (520 BC) and motivated Jerusalemites to reconstruct the temple of Jerusalem.

title: Hallelujah
description: A Hebrew expression meaning ‘praise the Lord’
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Hebrew
description: A name given to the Israelites (Gen 39:14,17; 41:12). The name is derived from Eber (Gen 10:24), the ancestor of Abraham. Hence the Hebrews are “sons of Eber” (Gen 10:21). Hebrew was also the language that the Israelites spoke before they adopted Aramaic. Hebrew was the language of the OT writings.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Hebron
description: A city to the south of Jerusalem (Gen 13:18; Num 13:22) known also as Kirjath-arba (Gen 23:2; Josh 14:15; 15:3). The burial place of the Jewish patriarchs and matriarchs is in the cave of Machpelah in Hebron (Gen 23:17-20).

title: Hermon
description: The snow-capped mountain of the Anti-Lebanon range marking the boundary of Palestine (Deut 3:8; 4:48; Josh 11:3,17; 13:11; 12:1).

title: Herod the Great
description: The king of Palestine (37 BC–AD 4) appointed and supported by Rome. The son of Antipater, an Idumean. Herod was a cruel and paranoid ruler, and Jesus was born in Bethlehem when Herod was the king in Jerusalem. After his death his kingdom was divided among three of his sons: Archelaus, Philip and Antipas

title: Herodians
description: A Jewish political party in Israel that supported the Herodian rulers under the Roman rule (Mk 3:6; 12:13; Mt 22:16; Lk 20:20).

title: Herodias
description: The daughter of Aristobulus and Bernice, who had married Herod Philip I and left her husband to be with Herod Antipas. John the Baptist reprimanded them for their marital infidelity (Mt 14:3-11; Mk 6:17-28; Lk 3:19).

title: Horeb
description: The mountain range of Sinai (Ex 3:1; 17:6; 33:6; Ps 106:19).

title: Hosea
description: One of the twelve minor prophets who was from the northern kingdom of Israel and exercised his ministry in the north.

title: Idumaea
description: The Greek form of Edom (Is 34:5-6; Ezek 35:15; 36:5).

title: India
description: The Book of Esther refers to ‘India’, speaking of the extent of the Persian kingdom (Esther 1:1; 8:9).

title: Isaiah
description: One of the three Major Prophets (740–700 BC).

title: Ishmael
description: Abraham’s son by Hagar (Gen 16:15; 17:23).

title: Israel
description: The name that Jacob received after his theophany at Peniel (Gen 32:28). Jacob’s twelve sons formed the twelve tribes of people known as ‘Israel’.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: James
description: There were two apostles with the name James, known as James the Greater (brother of John) and James the Less, son of Alphaeus (Mt 10:3; Mk 3:18; Lk 6:15). James the Greater was martyred in AD 44 by Herod Agrippa I (Acts 12:2). James, the head of the Jerusalem Church, was another person with the same name, qualified as the brother of the Lord (Gal 1:18-19; Acts 15:13; 21:18; 12:17; 1 Cor 15:7).

title: Jeremiah
description: One of the three Major Prophets (640–585 BC)

title: Jerico
description: The city first occupied by the Israelites as they entered their promised land (Josh 6). The name means ‘place of fragrance’.

title: Jerusalem
description: The capital of the united kingdom of Israel under King David. The name means the ‘foundation of peace’ although some interpret it as the ‘city of peace’. Jerusalem is also known as Salem, Ariel, Jebus, Zion, the ‘city of God’, the ‘holy city’, ‘the city of David’ (2 Sam 5:5- 9), ‘el-Khuds’ (= holy). It was believed to be the place chosen by Yahweh for himself on the earth

title: Jesus
description: The Greek form of the Hebrew name ‘Joshua’ which was originally ‘Hoshea’ (Num 13:8,16), but changed by Moses into ‘Jehoshua’ (Num 13:16) or ‘Joshua’. After the Exile it assumed the form ‘Jeshua’ whence the Greek form ‘Jesus’. This was the name given to the incarnate Son of God to highlight his salvific mission (Mt 1:21).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Jew
description: A name given first to those belonging to the tribe of Judah distinguishing them from the people of the other tribes known as Israelites (2 Kings 16:6; 25:25; Jer 32:12; 38:19; 40:11; 41:3). During the Babylonian exile and after, the name ‘Jew’ was extended REVELATION to the people of the tribes without distinction (Esth 3:6,10; Dan 3:8,12; Ezra 4:12; 5:1,5).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: John
description: One of the twelve apostles of Jesus. The fourth Gospel and three letters of the NT are attributed to him

title: Jonah
description: One of the twelve minor prophets, not a historical figure but rather the main character of the Book of Jonah.

title: Joppa
description: A town on the Mediterranean coast (Jonah 1:3; Acts 9:36-43) known today as Jaffa.

title: Joseph
description: One of Jacob’s sons who was sold to the Egyptians and who later became the governor of the land.

title: Joshua
description: The successor of Moses as the leader of Israel who led them into the promised land. His name meant ‘Yahweh is Saviour.’

title: Josiah
description: One of the illustrious kings of Judah who began the Deuteronomistic reform in Jerusalem and encouraged recording oral religious traditions into writing.

title: Jubilee
description: The great semi-centennial festival of the Hebrews (Lev 25) that marked the liberation of slaves, the restoration of sold properties, the temporary abandonment of farming, to acknowledge Yahweh as the real owner of their land and property.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Judah
description: One of Jacob’s sons and King David was from the tribe of Judah

title: Judas Iscariot
description: One of the twelve apostles of Jesus. who betrayed his Master (Jn 13:18-30; 18:2-3; Mk 14:43-50; Mt 26:14-16,47-50).

title: Judus Thaddeus
description: One of the twelve apostles of Jesus. (Lk 6:15; Mk 3:18)

title: Lazarus
description: Jesus’ friend and brother of Mary and Martha of Bethany (Jn 11:1- 44). Another man with the same name was a beggar named in the parable recorded in Lk 16:19-31.

title: Lebanon
description: A country on the north-eastern side of Israel on the coast of the Mediterranean Sea. Tyre and Sidon that are often mentioned in the Bible are now in Lebanon

title: Leviathan
description: A dragon, sometimes depicted as an enemy crushed by the divine power (Job 3:8; 41:1; Ps 104:26; Is 27:1).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Levirate Law
description: An ancient Jewish custom mandated by Law (Deut 25:5-10). This law required that when an Israelite man died without a child, his surviving brother (levir = a husband’s brother) should marry the widow in order to procreate a son in the name of the dead brother (Gen 38; Ruth 3–4).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Levite
description: A person belonging to the priestly tribe of Levi (Exod 6:25; Lev 25:32; Num 35:2).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Luke
description: A Gentile Christian physician who wrote the third Gospel and the Acts of the Apostles.

title: Lydia
description: The first person to become a Christian in Philippi (Acts 16:11-15).

title: Malachi
description: One of the twelve minor prophets

title: Malchus
description: A servant of the high priest Caiaphas, whose ear was cut off by Peter when his party came to arrest Jesus (Jn 18:10). Jesus cured it with a touch (Mt 26:51; Mk 14:47; Lk 22:51).

title: Manna
description: The food that God gave to the Israelites during their wanderings in the wilderness (Exod 16:15-35). The word manna from ‘man-hu’ means ‘What is that?’
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Maranatha
description: Two Aramean words, Maran’athah, meaning, “Come, O Lord” (1 Cor 16:22; Rev 22:20b).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Mark
description: One of the four evangelists who composed the Gospels.

title: Martha
description: The sister of Lazarus and Mary of Bethany (Jn 11:1-39; Lk 10:38-41).

title: Mary
description: Mother of Jesus and wife of Joseph (Mt 2:11; Lk 1:32; Acts 1:14).

title: Mary Cleopas
description: The wife of Cleopas (Jn 19:25) and probably the mother of James and Joses (Mt 27:56,61; Mk 15:40,47).

title: Mary Magdalene
description: Mary from Magdala who became Jesus’ devout disciple and followed him to his cross and tomb (Lk 8:1-3; Mt 27:55; Mk 15:41; Lk 23:55).

title: Mary of Bethaney
description: The sister of Lazarus and Martha (Jn 11:1-39; Jn 12:1-8; Mk 14:1-9; Mt 26:6-13).

title: Mary of the Cenacle
description: The mother of John Mark (Acts 12:12), and it was probably in her house that Jesus celebrated his Last Supper with his twelve apostles (Mk 14:12-31). She might have been Barnabas’ sister (Col 4:10; Acts 15:36-41).

title: Matthew
description: One of Jesus’ twelve apostles and one of the four evangelists in whose name is the first Gospel. He was also known as Levi (Mt 9:9; 2:14; Lk 5:27).

title: Megiddo
description: One of the fortified cities of Solomon (1 Kings 9:15)

title: Melchizedek
description: The king and the high priest of Salem who blessed Abraham and offered him bread and wine (Gen 14:18-20). As his patronymic is not found, he is seen symbolically as eternal high priest foreshadowing the eternal priesthood of Jesus (Ps 110:4; Heb 5:6; 6:20).

title: Mesopotamia
description: The Greek name of the country between the two rivers (Aramnaharaim in Hebrew), i.e. the region between the Euphrates and the Tigris (Gen 24:10; Deut 23:4; Judg 3:8-10).

title: Messiah
description: The English version of the Hebrew term “mashiah”, equivalent to the Greek word “Christos”. Both these mean ‘anointed’.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Micah
description: One of the twelve minor prophets.

title: Mizraim
description: The land of Egypt (Gen 10:6).

title: Moriah
description: The mount where Abraham was asked to sacrifice Isaac (Gen 22).

title: Moses
description: The liberator and the law-giver of the Israelites. The name means ‘drawn out of water’ (Exodus–Deuteronomy).

title: Nahum
description: One of the twelve minor prophets who prophesied in Judah before 612 BC.

title: Nathan
description: A court prophet of David who conveyed to David Yahweh’s promise (2 Sam 7) and who reprimanded David for his sin against Uriah (2 Sam 12). He played a key role in Solomon’s ascension to the throne of David (1 Kings 1).

title: Nathanael
description: One of the followers of Jesus from Cana (Jn 1:45-51; 21:2). As his name is mentioned only in the fourth Gospel which does not include Bartholomew in the list of the twelve apostles, scholars identify him with Bartholomew.

title: Nazareth
description: The village in Galilee where the Holy Family lived (Lk 2:39).

title: Nebo
description: A mountain in the land of Moab (the present kingdom of Jordan) from where Moses viewed the Promised Land for the first and the last time (Deut 32:49; 34:1). Now this place is reconstructed as a pilgrimage centre commemorating the death of Moses

title: Nicodemus
description: A Pharisee and a member of the Sanhedrin who visited Jesus at night (Jn 3:1-21; 7:50-52). He together with Joseph of Arimathea buried Jesus (Jn 19:38-42)

title: Nineveh
description: Once the flourishing capital of Assyria on the bank of the river Tigris (2 Kings 19:36; Is 37:37; Jonah 3–4).

title: Obadiah
description: One of the twelve minor prophets who prophesied after 587 BC

title: Palestine
description: The land of Canaan inhabited by the Philistines (Ex 15:14; Is 14:29,31; Joel 3:4).

title: Paphos
description: Once the capital of Cyprus; Paul and Barnabas visited this place on their first missionary journey (Acts 13:6).

title: Paradise
description: A Persian word meaning a ‘pleasure ground’, ‘park’ or ‘king’s garden’. The garden of Eden (Gen 2:8).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Passover
description: One of the three Jewish pilgrimage feasts (Exod 12–13; Lev 23:4-8; Deut 16:1-8).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Patmos
description: The island in the Aegean Sea to which John was banished by the Emperor Domitian (Rev 1:9).

title: Paul
description: The apostle of the Gentiles who evangelized extensively, establishing church communities in many places. He was the first writer of the NT books and his writings are known as Pauline epistles. He was one of the greatest missionaries and theologians of all times.

title: Persia
description: An ancient empire extending from the Indus to Thrace and from the Caspian Sea to the Red Sea and the Persian Gulf.

title: Peter
description: Simon Peter, the head of the apostles, brother of Andrew (Mk 1:16- 18; 16:17).

title: Pharaoh
description: The title of the rulers of Egypt. The word might mean ‘the sun-god’ or ‘his majesty.’
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Philemon
description: A slave master of Colossae who became a Christian and the head of the Christian community there (Col 4:9; Philem 1:2).

title: Philip
description: One of the twelve apostles (Mk 3:18; Mt 10:3; Jn 6:5-7; 12:21-22; 14:8-9; Acts 1:13). One of the seven deacons was also a person named Philip (Acts 6: 5; 21: 8-9).

title: Philippi
description: Once the capital of the province of Macedonia. Paul established a Christian community there (Acts 16:11-40). Paul had a great relationship with the Philippian Christians

title: Phoebe
description: A deaconess of the Church at Cenchrea (Rom 16:1-2).

title: Phoenicia
description: The land of palm trees, a country along the shores of the Mediterranean Sea, known today as Lebanon (Acts 21:2).

title: Phylacteries
description: Strips of parchment enclosed in a square leather case with inscriptions of Exod 13:1-10; 11-16; Deut 6: 4-9; 11:18-21. This case was fastened by straps to the forehead just between the eyes, and on the left arm near the elbow. Today they are known as tephilin (prayers)
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Pilate, Pontius
description: The Roman procurator of Judea from AD 26 to 36. It was he who condemned Jesus to death by crucifixion at the instigation of the Jewish leaders (Mk 15:1-15)

title: Praetorium
description: A Greek word denoting a judgement hall attached to a palace where the governor/ruler heard cases for judgement (Mk 15:16; Mt 27:27; Jn 18:28,33; 19:9; Acts 23:35)
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Priscilla
description: Wife of Aquila, both companions of Paul (Acts 18:18-26)

title: Prophecy
description: Mediation and interpretation of the mind and will of God by those who were divinely designated for the task.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Prophet
description: A person who went to the kings and to people, uninvited and fearless, announcing God’s judgement or mercy.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Proselyte
description: A convert to Judaism (Acts 2:11; 6:5; 13:43; Mt 23:15).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Publican
description: A tax collector like Zacchaeus (Lk 19:2) who levied taxes from people on behalf of the government.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Rabbi
description: A title of honor given to Jewish doctors of the law and distinguished teachers. The word means “my master/great”.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Raca
description: A word of contempt (Mt 5:22). The word means ‘vain, empty, worthless’.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Rameses
description: A place in Egypt, probably the land of Goshen (Gen 47:11; 45:10).

title: Ruth
description: A Moabitess who married Boaz of Bethlehem after her first husband Mahlon died in the land of Moab. She became the mother of Obed, the grandfather of David (cf. The Book of Ruth).

title: Sabaoth
description: A transliteration which means ‘hosts’ or ‘armies’. The usual Hebrew expression in the OT is ‘Yahweh Sabaoth’ with the meaning ‘Lord of hosts or armies’. ‘Hosts’ may mean all the heavenly bodies like stars or all the heavenly beings such as angels
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Sabbath
description: The seventh day of the week considered as the obligatory day of rest (Exod 20:8-11; Deut 5:12-15; Exod 35:2-3; Lev 23:3; 26:34). Jesus criticised the perverted notion of Sabbath (Mt 12:10-13; Mk 2:27; Lk 13:10-17).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Sabbatical Year
description: Every seventh year during which the land had to remain uncultivated (Lev 25:2-7; Exod 23:10-12; Lev 26:34-35)
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Sadducees
description: The Jewish sect, believed to be the followers of Zadok, mostly consisting of priests who did not accept beliefs in resurrection, angels, tradition, etc.

title: Samaria
description: A place north-west of Shechem. King Omri of Israel purchased this hill from Shemer for two talents of silver and built on its broad summit the new capital of his kingdom (1 Kings 16:24). The name of Samaria came from its former owner ‘Shemer’.

title: Samaritans
description: The mixed population of Assyrians and Jews that eventually adopted the Jewish religion.

title: Sanhedrin
description: The supreme judicial and administrative council of the Jews. The meaning of the word is ‘sitting together’ or ‘council’. The NT refers to it frequently (Mt 5:22; 26:59; Mk 15:1, etc.).

title: Scapegoat
description: The goat for Azazel sent away into the wilderness on the Day of Atonement/Yom Kippur, putting upon it all the sins of Israel (Lev 16:7-10)
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Scribes
description: Those who acted as secretaries of state whose duty it was to prepare and issue decrees in the name of the king (2 Sam 8:17; 20:25; 1 Chron 18:16; 24:6; 1 Kings 4:3; 2 Kings 12:9-11; 18:18-37, etc.). Later scribes were known as experts of Torah or the Jewish Law.

title: Sea of Galilee
description: Known by different names, such as the ‘sea of Kinnereth’ (Num 34:11; Josh 12:3; 13:27) as it is in the shape of a harp; the ‘lake of Gennesareth’ (Lk 5:1), and the ‘sea of Tiberias’ (Jn 6:1; 21:1). It is 212 meters below sea level, 54 meters deep, 21 kilometers long, 13 kms wide, and has clean water

title: Shechem
description: A city in Samaria (Gen 33:18) where the bones of Joseph were buried (Josh 24: 32), one of the cities of refuge (Josh 20:7). It was here that Rehoboam was made king (1 Kings 12:1,19).

title: Shiloh
description: A city of Ephraim to the north of Bethel. The first sanctuary of Israel was set up here to keep the ark (Josh 18:1-10).

title: Shinar
description: Another name for Babylon (Gen 11:1-6; 14:1; Is 11:11; Zech 5:11).

title: Showbread / Bread of the Presence
description: The twelve loaves of bread kept on a table in the Holy Place of the temple every Sabbath day (Lev 24:5-9; Exod 25:30; 1 Sam 21:1-6; Mt 12:3-4).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Sidon
description: A place in Phoenicia, i.e. today’s Lebanon (Gen 10:15,19; Mt 11:21- 22; Lk 6:17).

title: Smyrna
description: One of the seven churches of Asia Minor in today’s Turkey (Rev 2:8- 11).

title: Solomon
description: David’s son by Bathsheba, who succeeded him on his throne (1 Kings 1-11).

title: Stephan
description: One of the seven deacons and the first Christian martyr (Acts 6:1– 8:2).

title: Synagogue
description: A Jewish house of prayer that developed as a result of the exilic idea of creating individual houses of worship on the local level to save the Jewish identity despite the destruction of the Temple in 587 BC.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Syria
description: The country known as Aram in the OT.

title: Tabernacle
description: This term denotes several things: a) A dwelling place (Job 5:24; 18:6); b) A portable shrine (Amos 5:26); c) A sacred tent, i.e. the movable tent temple which Moses erected for the service of God (Exod 25:9; Heb 8:5). This tabernacle was made in such a way that it could easily be dismantled and moved from place to place during the wanderings in the wilderness.
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Tabor
description: The highest mountain of Jezreel about 11 miles west of the Sea of Galilee and the probable site of Jesus’ transfiguration (Mk 9:2-13).

title: Talent
description: A unit of weight and value in the Jewish society: 1 Talent = 60 kilograms in weight (1 Kings 9:14); 1 Talent = 60 x 60 x .8 = $ 2880 in value now (Mt 25:15).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Tarshish
description: Exact location is unknown; suggestions are: a port in Spain, Carthage in Africa, or a port in the Indian Ocean (1 Kings 9:26; 22:48).

title: Tarsus
description: The once main city of Cilicia, in the present Turkey; the birthplace of Paul (Acts 21:39)

title: Tetrarch
description: Ruler of one fourth part of a kingdom (Mt 14:1; Lk 3:1, 19; 9: 7; Acts 13:1).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Theophilus
description: The person to whom Luke dedicated both his Gospel (Lk 1:3) and the Acts of the Apostles (1:1).

title: Thessalonica
description: Once the capital of one of the four Roman districts of Macedonia, where Paul founded a Church and to whome Paul wrote letters

title: Thomas
description: One of the twelve apostles of Jesus. He is also called Didymus meaning ‘Twin’ (Mk 3:18; Jn 20:24; Mt 10:3). He went to India for evangelisation

title: Thummim
description: Urim and Thummim were possibly tablets of wood kept in the breastplate of the priest, and used to decide if someone was guilty or innocent. If the Urim was selected, it meant guilt, while selection of the Thummim would mean innocence (Exod 28:30; Deut 33:8; Judg 1: 1; 20:18; 1 Sam 14: 3, 18; 23:9; 2 Sam 21:1).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Tiberias
description: A city on the western shore of the Sea of Tiberias (Jn 6:1,23; 21:1).

title: Timothy
description: Paul’s disciple and companion (Acts 16:1-4)

title: Titus
description: Paul’s disciple and companion who accompanied him to the Jerusalem Council (Gal 2:1-3; Acts 15:2)

title: Ur
description: The place from where Abraham began his journey along with his father Terah and other kinsmen (Gen 11:31).

title: Zacchaeus
description: The tax collector of Jericho who was converted by the concern shown by Jesus (Lk 19:1-10).

title: Zealots
description: A sect of Jews who refused to pay tribute to the Romans on the ground that this was a violation of the principle that God was the only King of Israel (Acts 5:37).
category: TERMS AND EXPRESSIONS IN THE BIBLE

title: Zechariah
description: One of the twelve minor prophets. Another person with the same name was the father of John the Baptist.

title: Zephaniah
description: One of the twelve minor prophets (627–622 BC).

title: Zion
description: Another name of Jerusalem
